steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: Set each factor of $(5x+4)(2x-5)=0$ to zero: $5x+4=0$ gives $x=-\tfrac{4}{5}$, and $2x-5=0$ gives $x=\tfrac{5}{2}$.

steps.title: Identify the matching solution
steps.content: The solutions are $-\tfrac{4}{5}$ and $\tfrac{5}{2}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
